pack_meta.title: Unified Methodology Pack: 10 Mobility Dimensions × Exit/Return + 7 Agency Parameters + Gap Closure Layer + Residual Gap Deep Research
merges: deep_research_v0_5_findings (this conversation: 16-source verification + 8 additional sources + 5 attribution corrections + contextual updates)

scope.framework: 10 dimensions (5 axes × exit/return) + 7 agency parameters + 7 derived gap-closing indicators

positioning.recommended_claim: An integrating operational architecture rather than a wholly new construct.
defensible_novelty: explicit separation of exit capacity and return capacity within each axis (10 dimensions)
multi-axis treatment across geographic, vertical, lateral/professional, industry, and employment-form mobility

individual-level agency framing that links psychological constructs (CAAS, CCQ, PCAS/BCAS) to structural labour-market data (LEHD J2J, BLS CWS, Eurostat LFS-Long, Cedefop Skills-OVATE) via 7 derived gap-closing indicators
explicit A/B/C/D evidence classification at the source and claim level
explicit Postановление № 792 / Приказ Минтруда № 359 dual-layer treatment of Russian credential portability (de-fanging vs. content methodology)
avoid_claims: do not claim that no prior work measures career mobility at all
do not claim that all dimensions are completely new
do not treat country-level indices such as Cedefop ESI as individual-level agency instruments
do not describe derived indicators (I01-I07) as validated instruments — they are operational proxies awaiting validation
do not merge CN/RU pseudo-task-vectors into O*NET/PIAAC data without confidence penalties
do not claim that Baldi-Trigeorgis 2020 is an individual-level option-value instrument — it is a Fortune-500 case study at organization/SHRM level (J of Intellectual Capital 21(5))

do not anchor I07_ICAS at A-level for CN/RU — none of the four patch instruments (CFI-R, Work Capability Set, CARINAS, Career Sustainability Scale) has documented psychometric invariance for those jurisdictions; bottleneck rule caps confidence at C for CN/RU

overall_confidence_by_country.note_v05: Confidence levels unchanged from v0.4. Deep research downgraded several individual instruments but did not change country-level overall confidence.

evidence_classification.definition_source: project convention, applied consistently across packs

A.label: primary
A.description: primary peer-reviewed / official registry / pre-registered RCT / national statistics with published methodology
examples: Akkermans et al. 2013 CCQ (Journal of Career Development)

B.label: strong_observational
B.description: observational / quasi-experimental / large survey / well-attested administrative data; non-experimental but methodologically rigorous
examples: LEHD Job-to-Job Flows (Census + state UI data)

C.label: practitioner
C.description: practitioner literature / corporate self-disclosure / industry survey / non-peer-reviewed but widely used
examples: Benko-Weisberg 2007 Mass Career Customization (HBR Press, Deloitte)
Luehrman 1998 'Strategy as a Portfolio of Real Options' (HBR — practitioner-strategic framework)
Tech companies' dual ladder programs (Shopify, Atlassian, Walmart)

D.label: concept_or_analogy
D.description: opinion / forecast / pure concept / theoretical analogy without operational instrument
examples: Real options as analogy for career decisions (without instrument)
Bandura social-cognitive agency applied to career exit/return without domain-specific scale
abstract claim that some phenomenon 'can be measured' without proposed operationalisation

evidence_classification.application_rule: Each source in datasources_unified, each field neighbour, each gap-closure signal carries an evidence_level field. When a derived indicator combines multiple sources of mixed evidence levels, the indicator's confidence inherits from the weakest essential signal (the bottleneck rule).

summary.verdict: v0.3 gap closure pack passes fact-check at HIGH CONFIDENCE. All 22 sources in source_registry are correctly identified and accurately characterized. Two refinements proposed: (1) add the Russian Moscow PIAAC exclusion as a comparability penalty in CR_04; (2) note the 2024 weakening of Russian profstandards binding status (Постановление Правительства № 792).

v03_source_verifications.primary_findings: BLS CWS July 2023 confirmed: 6.9M contingent workers (4.3% of employed, up from 3.8% in May 2017); 7.4% independent contractors; 1.7% on-call; 0.6% temp help; 0.5% contract firm workers; 16.3M (10.1%) in alternative arrangements; 8.4M multiple jobholders (5.2%); CWS history: 1995/97/99/2001/2005/2017/2023, next May 2025; ~60K households monthly CPS sample. 2023 added digital platform / app-based work questions and second-job questions.

Census LEHD Job-to-Job Flows: quarterly data based on linked UI records + QCEW; series since 2000Q2, latest national release 2023Q4 (R2025Q1); state and MSA level. Critical limitation: excludes self-employed and federal defense employees — relevant for employment_form_exit_return dimension. Multiple measures: J2J transition rate, hires/separations, earnings changes, origin/destination job characteristics.
limitations_to_add: excludes self-employed individuals — direct cap on usefulness for employment_form transitions
multiplicative noise infusion (fuzz factor) on all releases for confidentiality

v03_source_verifications.primary_findings: Akkermans, Brenninkmeijer, Huibers, Blonk (2013), Journal of Career Development 40(3): 245-267. Three-dimension framework (reflective/communicative/behavioral) × 6 competencies: reflection on motivation, reflection on qualities, networking, self-profiling, work exploration, career control. Initially validated on Dutch young employees age 16-30 (two samples). Six-factor structure with second-order common factor. Good content, factorial, discriminant, and incremental validity.
caveats_to_add: Original validation sample was young employees (16-30) — generalisability to mid-career exit/return populations needs additional validation
Strong instrument for A2 (career_identity) and A3 (adaptability_self_efficacy) and A4 (network_capital)

v03_source_verifications.primary_findings: Luehrman T.A. (1998). 'Strategy as a Portfolio of Real Options.' Harvard Business Review 76(5), Sept-Oct 1998, pp. 89-99. Paired with 'Investment Opportunities as Real Options' (HBR July-Aug 1998). Two-metric Option Space + 'tomato garden' metaphor. Foundational practitioner-strategic real-options paper.
caveats_to_add: Strategic-investment context, not careers — Luehrman is rhetorical predecessor for OV (option value) thinking, NOT direct operational instrument for individual career return options

For career real-options literature specifically, more relevant: Bowman-Hurry 1993 (option lens for strategic investments under uncertainty); McGrath 1997 (real options and strategic investment); Foote-Folta 2002 (real options applied to firm-level human capital). Career-individual-level real options remains conceptual.

v03_source_verifications.primary_findings: Raelin J.A. (1987). 'Two-Track Plans for One-Track Careers.' Personnel Journal 66, January 1987, pp. 96-101. Foundational practitioner article on dual ladder (technical track + managerial track with comparable prestige, salary, status). Now widely implemented in tech: Shopify (crafters/managers), Google, Atlassian, Walmart Global Tech, Microsoft, Meta. Operationalisation varies across firms.
caveats_to_add: C-level evidence: practitioner journal, not peer-reviewed
Construct widely cited but no single validated psychometric scale measures the worker's perception of dual-ladder availability

Modern academic treatment: Allen-Katz 1986 'Dual ladders revisited' (Research-Technology Management); Petroni-Colacino 2008 (R&D Management). Academic work confirms continued relevance and identifies failure modes (poor parity, prestige asymmetry).

v03_source_verifications.primary_findings: Реестр профессиональных стандартов Минтруда России. ~1,263+ active профстандартов (2019 figure; current larger). Maintained by ФГБУ 'ВНИИ труда' Минтруда России. Each profstandard contains: трудовые функции (job functions), требуемые знания/умения (required knowledge/skills), уровни квалификации (qualification levels). Foundation in TK RF (Federal Law No. 236-FZ from 03.12.2012).
v03_source_verifications.critical_finding_for_v04: On 13 June 2024, Постановление Правительства РФ № 792 came into force: при осуществлении государственного контроля (надзора) больше не допускается оценка соблюдения требований профстандартов как обязательных, и несоблюдение требований профстандартов не может являться основанием для административной ответственности (with narrow exceptions, e.g. accountants under Federal Law No. 402-FZ). This SIGNIFICANTLY weakens profstandards as a credential/regulatory barrier in Russia. They remain useful as occupation-content layer (verbs, functions, knowledge) for pseudo-vector construction (gap_closure cn_ru_comparability), but should NOT be treated as a binding credential portability mechanism.
v03_source_verifications.implication_for_pack: In gap_closures.option_value_of_return.OV_03_switching_cost and gap_closures.cn_ru_comparability.IR_04, soften how profstandards are used: occupation-content yes; binding credential rule no (for most professions, post June 2024).

v03_source_verifications.primary_findings: Survey of Income and Program Participation — longitudinal household survey by US Census Bureau. Each panel ~14,000-52,000 households, followed for 4 years. Captures detailed employment, earnings, transitions, self-employment. Foundational US source for individual-panel transition analysis. Latest panel: 2022 panel.

Eurostat EU-LFS longitudinal microdata: 2-quarter and 4-quarter panel constructions tracking labour-market transitions across 27 EU member states + EFTA. Standard dataset code lfsi_long, lfsi_jht. Quarterly transitions between employment/unemployment/inactivity. Limitations: short panels (2-4 quarters) limit detection of long-term return cycles; sample size varies by country; self-employment status changes captured but with low granularity.

Cedefop Skills-OVATE: online job ads aggregation across 32 European countries (27 EU + EFTA + UK), launched 2015, quarterly updates. Powered by Cedefop + Eurostat Web Intelligence Hub. Hundreds of millions of OJAs analyzed. Cross-walks ISCO-08 / NACE rev.2 / NUTS-2 / ESCO v1 / O*NET. Demand-side near-real-time data for occupations and skills.
caveats_to_add: Online-only — under-represents informal hiring channels; some sectors over-represented (tech, professional services), others under-represented (agriculture, manual trades)
Snapshot of demand (vacancies), not realized hires

v03_source_verifications.primary_findings: Baldi 2012/2013 work on real options applied to human-capital career development. Conceptual extension of Luehrman framework to individual career investment decisions (training, occupation choice). Primarily theoretical; no validated individual-level instrument.
v03_source_verifications.rationale_for_D: Theoretical analogy without operationalisation at individual level; useful as conceptual scaffolding for OV indicator but not as data source.

v03_source_verifications.primary_findings: Jacobs 'How to transition from academia to industry and back' literature exists in scattered form (Nature Careers columns, ASAPbio guides, AAAS Science Careers, individual academic blogs). No single canonical Jacobs reference identified — likely refers to the broader academia↔industry transition literature including Mangematin 2000 (Research Policy on PhDs in industry), Roach-Sauermann 2010 (Research Policy on academic vs industry careers), and Gaughan-Robin 2004 (PhD careers).
edits_suggested: Either pin a specific Jacobs reference (year, journal) OR rename source to SRC_ACADEMIA_INDUSTRY_TRANSITION_LITERATURE pointing to a basket of references (Mangematin 2000, Roach-Sauermann 2010, Lam 2007 R&D Management on hybrid academic-industry roles)

v03_source_verifications.primary_findings: Anand-Hunter-Smith 2005 'The measurement of capabilities' — Sen-Nussbaum capability approach operationalised on British Household Panel Survey (BHPS). Cited correctly in v0.3.

ten_mobility_dimensions.description: Capacity to leave current geography for another labor market.

canonical_sources.role: US individual panel of geographic transitions

US.notes: LEHD J2J + ACS migration + NBER hazard models give strong individual-level signal

EU.notes: LFS + EU-SILC give intra-EU mobility; ESCO + EQF + Lisbon RC support credential portability

CN.notes: Internal migration data via population census; international outflow harder to measure individually

RU.notes: Rosstat outmigration data limited; post-2022 emigration flows poorly observable; OECD Migration Outlook is best external proxy

ten_mobility_dimensions.residual_gap: Individual-level exit-readiness scoring (vs. realized flows) requires survey augmentation; no off-the-shelf instrument.

ten_mobility_dimensions.description: Capacity to return to prior geography or origin labor market.

canonical_sources.role: Amanzadeh-Kermani-McQuade 2024: 38% return within 10 years; asymmetric exposure to negative firm growth
RU occupation content for matching foreign qualifications domestically
CN occupation directory for repatriating Chinese workers

US.notes: Strong NBER-style empirical anchor; LEHD return-to-prior-state observable in some panels; iRelaunch C-level data for career-break return

EU.notes: Lisbon RC + EQF give credential return mechanism; LFS-Long tracks short-cycle returns

CN.notes: Returnee literature qualitative; Tokyo Convention 2011/2018 gives credential mechanism; '海归' (haigui) corpus large but methodologically weak

RU.notes: Lisbon RC ratified (2000) but practical operation degraded post-March 2022 (Russia ceased CoE membership); profstandards weakened as binding rule (Постановление № 792 от 13 июня 2024). Empirical return-migration data sparse and methodologically constrained.

ten_mobility_dimensions.residual_gap: Individual-level return-capacity score requires combining hazard-model baseline + survey add-on + credential-portability check. Asymmetric exposure to destination shocks (Amanzadeh-Kermani-McQuade) is empirically established but not turned into individual instrument.

ten_mobility_dimensions.description: Capacity to move up, down, or out of current hierarchical track (manager/IC/expert ladders).

canonical_sources.role: Job Zones (4-level since 30.2 Feb 2026) for level mapping

US.notes: Strong O*NET level mapping; LEHD earnings quartile transitions; tech-sector dual ladder C-level prevalent

EU.notes: ESCO ISCO-08 levels; weaker dual-ladder formalisation outside tech

CN.notes: Occupation dictionary classification levels exist; SOE vs. private firm vertical structures heterogeneous

RU.notes: OKZ ISCO-08 alignment + profstandards qualification levels; weak dual-ladder culture outside multinational corporations and IT

ten_mobility_dimensions.residual_gap: Vertical exit at individual level (ready/willing/able to step up, down, or out) requires survey add-on combining career identity (CCQ) + level recency (O*NET) + market liquidity (LEHD/Skills-OVATE).

ten_mobility_dimensions.description: Capacity to re-enter prior seniority, management, or IC track after a track switch (manager→IC→manager, IC→manager→IC, post-break re-entry to senior level).

canonical_sources.role: track-switch flow rates and earnings outcomes

US.notes: Tech-sector dual ladder culturally normalized (Shopify crafters, Google IC ladder); 7 signals all operationalisable

EU.notes: Less dual-ladder culture outside tech; some sectors strongly hierarchical

CN.notes: Tech sectors (Alibaba, Tencent, ByteDance) have IC tracks; weaker outside tech

RU.notes: Limited dual-ladder outside multinationals; track-switch stigma reportedly higher than in US

ten_mobility_dimensions.residual_gap: Major gap. No off-the-shelf measure for return-after-track-switch. Closure path: combine VR_01 dual ladder availability (Raelin C-level) + VR_02 level equivalence (O*NET A-level) + VR_03 recency (O*NET + CCQ) + VR_04 evidence portfolio + VR_05 market liquidity + VR_06 identity fit + VR_07 stigma — see I06_VRCS.

ten_mobility_dimensions.description: Capacity to move into adjacent or distant occupations based on transferable skills and tasks.

US.notes: O*NET 30.2 + Robinson 2018 + Bechichi et al. 2018-2019 give strongest infrastructure globally

EU.notes: ESCO 3,039 × 13,939 × 28 languages; multilingual is unique advantage

CN.notes: 1,639 professions (MoHRSS 2022) + 158 new since 2015 + 97 digital. NO state-published task-vector layer; Xu et al. 2020 (arXiv 2001.02863) academic prototype exists

RU.notes: OKZ ISCO-08 alignment + profstandards content; no task-vector layer; pseudo-vector via NLP on profstandards text feasible (gap_closure cn_ru_comparability)

ten_mobility_dimensions.residual_gap: Methodologically mature for US/EU; CN/RU need pseudo-vector construction with confidence penalties (see I04_CCS).

ten_mobility_dimensions.description: Capacity to return to prior occupation after occupational switch.

canonical_sources.role: occupational switch and return flow rates
skill decay / re-entry skill gap measurement
individual return readiness via career identity + work exploration

US.notes: Skill decay observable in O*NET vector recency + LEHD occupation flow rates

EU.notes: ESCO + Lisbon RC for credentials; LFS-Long short panels limit return detection

RU.notes: Pseudo-vector + profstandards content; Lisbon RC nominally available but practical operation constrained post-2022

ten_mobility_dimensions.residual_gap: Lateral-professional return is consistently the weakest dimension across all four countries. Individual-level instrument would require: occupation pair distance × skill decay × credential renewal cost × demand for return target. None of these are bundled in a ready instrument.

ten_mobility_dimensions.description: Capacity to transfer skills and networks into another industry.

canonical_sources.role: skill demand by NACE rev.2 sector

US.notes: LEHD industry flows + O*NET task vectors; Aufiero et al. 2024 (Sci Rep) job-fitness/skill-coherence into wages

CN.notes: Industrial classification exists; flow data limited

RU.notes: OKVED industry codes; transition data via Rosstat aggregates only

ten_mobility_dimensions.residual_gap: Demand-side network specificity (sector-specific weak ties) is the missing piece — Akkermans CCQ touches on networking but not industry-specific.

ten_mobility_dimensions.description: Capacity to re-enter prior industry after exit (academia↔industry, finance↔tech, public↔private, regulated↔unregulated transitions).

canonical_sources.role: CN credential / occupation barriers by sector
academia↔industry transition literature (basket of references — see fact_check_v03)

US.notes: LEHD flow asymmetry + signal preservation observable; academia↔industry literature sparse but present

EU.notes: Skills-OVATE + LFS-Long; cross-country EU heterogeneity high

CN.notes: Public-private transition literature (Hu Lin et al.) qualitative

RU.notes: Limited inter-sector transition data; profstandards weakened post-June 2024 as regulatory barrier

ten_mobility_dimensions.residual_gap: MAJOR. Asymmetric industry re-entry (especially academia↔industry, finance↔tech, public↔private, regulated↔unregulated) is the weakest measured dimension after employment-form return. I03_IRAS proposes proxy from 6 signals but indicator is not validated. Residual gap is high severity.

ten_mobility_dimensions.description: Capacity to move from salaried employment into freelance, gig, founder, contractor, or portfolio work.

canonical_sources.role: US contingent + alternative work arrangements; 6.9M contingent (4.3%); 7.4% independent contractors
US individual panel of form switches

US.notes: BLS CWS 2023 (next May 2025) + SIPP + LEHD give strong baseline; LEHD excludes self-employed — important caveat

CN.notes: Platform-work and gig economy literature growing; 97 digital-economy professions in 2022 dictionary

RU.notes: Самозанятые (self-employed under НПД tax regime since 2019) ~10M+ on FNS register; transitions to/from employee status observable in tax data but not consolidated in single labor-market panel

ten_mobility_dimensions.residual_gap: LEHD's exclusion of self-employed is the structural blind spot in the US. The 'inferred from status changes in CPS basic monthly survey + administrative tax data + CWS supplement' triangulation is feasible but not bundled.

ten_mobility_dimensions.description: Capacity to return from freelance, gig, founder, contractor, or portfolio work into salaried employment.

canonical_sources.role: individual panel; rare ready evidence on independent→employee return
destination employer characteristics for those entering payroll from non-payroll states (limited by self-employed exclusion)
rhetorical predecessor for option-value framing only

US.notes: iRelaunch returnship statistics (~70+ employer programs, ~80% returnship-to-hire rate, C-level corporate self-disclosure) PARTIALLY closes employment_form_return for career-break case. CWS + SIPP + LEHD triangulation is feasible but not bundled.

EU.notes: LFS-Long + EU-SILC + EWCS; ESCO credential recognition; no equivalent to iRelaunch programmatic infrastructure

CN.notes: Platform-to-employee return literature minimal; no tracking infrastructure

RU.notes: Самозанятые → employee transitions observable in tax data; не консолидировано как panel

ten_mobility_dimensions.residual_gap: MAXIMAL gap (severity: high). Together with D08 (industry_return) and D04 (vertical_return), the three return dimensions form the framework's hardest measurement frontier. Closure path: I01_EFBCS bidirectional capacity score combining transition propensity + income continuity + skill continuity + market demand for return job + survey add-on (S1_form_reversibility 3 items + S2_return_option_assets 3 items). I02_ROVS adds option-value layer (probability × payoff × switching cost × time decay × volatility × reversibility assets).

agency_parameters.name: Skills and human capital
operational_signals: PIAAC-style adaptive problem-solving where coverage exists
agency_parameters.scoring_hint: Normalize individual skill vector against target occupation/industry/geography demands
agency_parameters.moscow_caveat: PIAAC Russian Cycle 1 sample EXCLUDED Moscow municipal area — under-represents the high-skill segment most relevant to this framework

agency_parameters.name: Career identity and knowing-why
agency_parameters.v02_lineage_correction: DeFillippi-Arthur 1994 (boundaryless career, JOB) + Arthur-Claman-DeFillippi 1995 (intelligent enterprise/intelligent career, AME 9(4)) — the 'three ways of knowing' (knowing-why/how/whom) trio crystallises in the 1995 paper
agency_parameters.scoring_hint: Survey/scale-based; do not infer reliably from labour statistics alone

agency_parameters.name: Adaptability and self-efficacy
agency_parameters.rtw_se_caveat: RTW-SE validated only in CLINICAL contexts (sick leave for mental disorders, musculoskeletal disease, post-surgery). For non-clinical exit/return-after-career-break scenarios, RTW-SE is conceptually relevant but not directly validated — domain-specific self-efficacy for return needs new validation.
agency_parameters.scoring_hint: Use CAAS/CCQ-style survey items; domain-specific self-efficacy for return should be added

agency_parameters.name: Network and social capital
agency_parameters.scoring_hint: Network capital is often location/industry-specific; score it separately for each exit/return path

agency_parameters.name: Credential and recognition portability
agency_parameters.scoring_hint: Treat as a CONVERSION FACTOR (in capability-approach sense), not as personal motivation

country_specific.EU: Lisbon Recognition Convention (1997, 57 Parties as of 1 July 2025) + EQF + Bologna
country_specific.US: no equivalent multilateral framework; state-level licensing dominates
country_specific.RU: Lisbon RC ratified 2000 but practical operation degraded post-March 2022 (Russia ceased CoE membership). Profstandards weakened as binding rule per Постановление № 792 от 13 июня 2024 — content-only role from then on for most professions.

agency_parameters.name: Labor-market liquidity and opportunity structure
agency_parameters.scoring_hint: Liquidity is location/occupation-specific; aggregate liquidity is a poor proxy for individual mobility opportunity

agency_parameters.name: Institutional, legal, and professional-closure constraints
agency_parameters.v02_terminology_correction: Original v0.2 used 'Freidson professional closure' — terminologically loose. Freidson's signature concepts are autonomy / dominance / Third Logic. Closure as a concept traces to Larson 1977 and Witz 1992 in the Weberian tradition. v0.4 separates the two attributions.
agency_parameters.scoring_hint: Treat as a HARD CAP / GATE rather than additive signal — geometric mean or multiplicative cap is methodologically preferable (per v0.3 schema aggregation_warning)

field_neighbors.name: Briscoe-Hall Protean / Boundaryless Career Attitudes Scales
key_difference: does not separate exit from return
does not cover all five mobility axes
trait malleability has been demonstrated empirically (Supeli & Creed 2016, Waters et al.) — methodologically relevant when plugging into dynamic exit/return measurement
field_neighbors.use_in_pack: primary psychological comparator; touches D03/D04 (vertical) and D09/D10 (employment-form)

field_neighbors.name: Career Adapt-Abilities Scale
field_neighbors.psychometric_properties: α=0.92 total; metric invariance across 13 countries; residual/strict invariance NOT achieved (France and Taiwan show poorer fit)
field_neighbors.use_in_pack: validated adaptability comparator for A3; touches all 10 dimensions indirectly via individual adaptability

field_neighbors.name: DeFillippi-Arthur Intelligent Career / Career Capital
key_difference: exit/return asymmetry is implicit at best
the 'three ways of knowing' trio crystallises in the 1995 paper, not the 1994 paper
field_neighbors.use_in_pack: conceptual ancestor of portability thinking; foundation for A1/A2/A4

field_neighbors.name: Sustainable Careers Model
field_neighbors.year_canonical: 2020 (JVB 117) for dimensions; 2005 (Van der Heijden) for outcomes
key_difference: dimensions (person/context/time) from De Vos-Van der Heijden-Akkermans 2020; outcomes triad (happiness/health/productivity) from Van der Heijden 2005 — explicitly cited as 'cf. Van der Heijden 2005' in De Vos et al. 2020
field_neighbors.use_in_pack: macro-theoretical comparator for the integration argument; the unified pack's positioning explicitly distinguishes its 10×7 decomposition from the more abstract person/context/time × happiness/health/productivity scaffolding of Sustainable Careers

field_neighbors.name: Mass Career Customization / Corporate Lattice
key_difference: does not measure exit beyond firm
C-level evidence: corporate self-disclosure with limited quantitative validation outside Deloitte's internal data
field_neighbors.use_in_pack: closest HR-practitioner comparator; touches D03/D04 (vertical) and D09/D10 (employment-form within firm)

field_neighbors.name: Cedefop European Skills Index
key_difference: covers EU-27 + UK, Iceland, Norway, Switzerland
field_neighbors.use_in_pack: methodological template for composite index construction, NOT a direct competitor; provides EU country-context layer for A6 (liquidity) and skills-system framing

field_neighbors.name: Robinson / OECD Bechichi occupational skill-distance methodology
field_neighbors.year_canonical: Robinson 2018 (JHR 53(2): 513-551); Bechichi et al. 2018 (OECD STI 52); 2019 (OECD STI 70)
key_difference: does not cover geographic, vertical, industry, or employment-form axes
field_neighbors.use_in_pack: strongest quantitative lateral-mobility comparator; foundation for D05 / D06 + I04_CCS
field_neighbors.extension_2024: Aufiero, De Marzo, Sbardella, Zaccaria 2024 (Sci Rep 14:11752) — economic-complexity-inspired extension applying job-fitness/skill-coherence to wages on O*NET. Replaces the v0.2 misattribution to 'Pugliese et al.'

field_neighbors.name: Brain circulation / skilled migration and return-migration literature
field_neighbors.key_findings: 38% of skilled migrants return within 10 years (33% within 5 years; 10% within 1 year). Return migration is asymmetrically exposed to negative firm employment growth (declines have substantially larger impact on return than equivalent growth has on staying). Returns to a year of US experience are 59-204% higher than a year in origin country for emerging-economy workers.
key_difference: main empirical anchor for exit/return ASYMMETRY argument across all axes (by extension)
field_neighbors.use_in_pack: main evidence base for D02 (geographic_return); the asymmetric-exposure finding generalises rhetorically to other axes but not empirically

datasources_unified.deep_research_correction: Patch labeled evidence_level B/C — deep research DOWNGRADES to C. Reason: organization-level case study with no individual-level instrument validation. Should be cited as conceptual / theoretical support for I02_ROVS framing, NOT as supporting evidence for an individual return-option-value scale. Replace as primary I02 anchor with Failla-Melillo-Reichstein 2017 JBV (admin data) + Hirschi-Wilhelm 2025 CSM-C (individual scale).

datasources_unified.deep_research_note: Patch listed only 4 of 5 subscales — Work-Life Balance subscale was missing. Now corrected. Validation samples are US college students; Park et al. 2019 measurement invariance across general vs. client samples — both still US. Cross-cultural validation for RU/CN: UNTESTED.

datasources_unified.name: Capability Set for Work questionnaire

datasources_unified.deep_research_caveat: Validated in Dutch working population only; cross-jurisdiction invariance not established. Subsequent literature (Brouwer et al.) extends to occupational-health populations, not labor-mobility specifically.

datasources_unified.name: Career Inaction Scale (CARINAS)
datasources_unified.scope: 8 items; four studies (Belgian + US samples; total N≈1,425). Theoretical antecedent: Verbruggen & De Vos (2020) AMR 45(2): 376-394 — but the scale paper is D'Huyvetter et al. 2025.
datasources_unified.deep_research_correction: Patch attributed authorship to 'Verbruggen & De Vos' but the scale paper is D'Huyvetter, Verbruggen, Jing & Akkermans 2025. Verbruggen-De Vos 2020 AMR is the THEORY paper without an instrument. Reverse-coding interpretation as proxy for career agency is reasonable but indirect — career inaction and career agency are conceptually related but not inverse constructs. Treat as B-level evidence for I07_ICAS, not direct A-level measure of agency.

datasources_unified.deep_research_caveat: Single-country (South Korea) validation. Cross-cultural invariance unstudied. Affiliations: Chung-Ang University.

datasources_unified.deep_research_correction: Patch cited only the 2020 arXiv preprint (arXiv:2001.02863). The peer-reviewed version is Xu et al. 2021 in Nature HSS Comm. Use 2021 as primary, arXiv 2020 as secondary. Explicitly NOT the official Chinese government skill taxonomy — China's official source remains the Ministry of Human Resources & Social Security's Occupation Classification Code (NOCC) + GBT/T 4754 industry codes, which are text-only without skill-vector embeddings.

datasources_unified.name: Приказ Минтруда РФ от 18 июля 2024 г. № 359
datasources_unified.title_full: Об утверждении методических рекомендаций по разработке и актуализации профессионального стандарта

datasources_unified.deep_research_critical_finding: Order № 359 regulates HOW to develop/update profstandards — does NOT restore mandatory/binding force. Postановление Правительства № 792 (12 June 2024) earlier removed обязательные требования профстандартов from regulatory-guillotine exclusion list. Letter Минтруда от 11 июля 2024 N 14-3/ООГ-4236 confirms: несоблюдение профстандартов не может быть основанием для административной ответственности. Use profstandards as content-NLP layer for I04_CCS_RU; do NOT use as binding credential portability mechanism.

datasources_unified.scope: Adopted under Article 8 of the Statute of the Council of Europe. Russia ceases to be a member effective 16 March 2022. Russia ceased to be a Party to the ECHR on 16 September 2022 (CM Resolution of 23 March 2022).
datasources_unified.deep_research_note: Resolution language is 'ceases to be a member' — NOT 'expulsion' or 'exclusion' procedurally. Verfassungsblog notes Russia withdrew on 15 March before formal cessation on 16 March. Operative legal status unambiguous. Belarus relations suspended 17 March 2022 (CM/Del/Dec(2022)1429/2.5).

datasources_unified.name: Lisbon Recognition Convention Committee Declaration restricting RU/BY participation
datasources_unified.date: 28 February 2023 (extraordinary session in Paris, announced 2 March 2023)
datasources_unified.scope: Restricts: (a) RU/BY candidates cannot be elected as Bureau member, Chair, Vice-Chair, working-group chair, rapporteur, or coordinator; (b) Secretariats advised of restriction on RU/BY representatives' participation in LRCC meetings. Does NOT terminate RU/BY treaty membership in the Lisbon Recognition Convention itself.
datasources_unified.deep_research_correction: Patch listed 2023 as ID (correct year for declaration). Critical distinction: committee work participation is RESTRICTED but treaty membership is NOT terminated. Russia ratified LRC in 2000 and remains formally a Party. ENIC-NARIC operational evaluation of Russian credentials in EU continues but with degraded throughput. Belarus is also a Party (non-CoE-member ratifier). Upstream CM decisions: 30 June and 5 October 2022.

datasources_unified.deep_research_caveat: Author list confirmation needed via direct DOI fetch — likely Hoffmann + Lechner + Kreutzer based on contemporaneous German R&D-management cluster, but the v0.5 assembler should verify by fetching the doi.org/10.1111/jpim.12310 record. Petroni-Colacino 2008 (J Tech Mgmt Innov 3(3): 21-32) is the seminal critique; Allen-Katz 1986 R&D Management is the foundational early critique — both should be cited as background.

derived_indicators.validation_target: observed employee↔self-employed/independent/payroll transitions in CPS-CWS, SIPP, LFS-Long, EU-SILC
derived_indicators.confidence: medium for US (CWS+SIPP+LEHD bundle); medium for EU (LFS-Long+SILC+EWCS); low for CN/RU
derived_indicators.construction_principle: Geometric mean preferred over additive: structural bottlenecks (legal access, regulated profession barriers) should cap rather than be averaged out
derived_indicators.validation_strategy: predictive validity → outcome validity → cross-country invariance → sensitivity analysis (per v0.3 implementation_notes)
derived_indicators.v05_warning: Existing panels can observe transitions; capacity and comparable-return quality still need survey/linked data.

derived_indicators.primary_dimension: all return dimensions: D02, D04, D06, D08, D10
derived_indicators.confidence: medium-low overall — rhetorical predecessors (Luehrman 1998 C, Baldi D) do not give individual-level instrument
derived_indicators.evidence_level_overall: B for objective realized option-value (admin data); C for subjective option-value perception
derived_indicators.warning: Do not treat return probability as return value — probability of return can be high because the destination failed, not because return is attractive (per v0.3 implementation_notes.do_not_overclaim)
derived_indicators.validation_strategy: predictive validity primarily; option-value as latent construct requires structural model
derived_indicators.v05_formula_stub: P_return × Payoff_quality × Reversibility_assets × Volatility_value - Switching_cost - Time_decay - Credential_decay
derived_indicators.v05_deep_research_correction: Baldi-Trigeorgis 2020 downgraded to C-level conceptual (firm/HRM, not individual). PRIMARY admin-data anchor: Failla-Melillo-Reichstein 2017 JBV (Danish IDA). Supplement: Mahieu-Melillo-Reichstein-Thompson 2021 SEJ. Individual-level scale anchor: Hirschi-Wilhelm 2025 CSM-C (replaces Baldi as I02 individual anchor).

derived_indicators.name: IndustryReentryAsymmetryScore
derived_indicators.output_scale: signed; positive = easier exit than return; absolute value = friction
derived_indicators.confidence: medium for US (LEHD strong); low-medium for EU/CN/RU

derived_indicators.name: ComparabilityConfidenceScore
bridge_strategy: anchor all countries to ISCO-08 / SOC / ESCO / OKZ
create task/skill pseudo-vectors for CN/RU from occupation dictionaries + profstandards + job ads
separate measurement comparability from labor-market comparability (credentials + legal + market are conversion factors)
derived_indicators.moscow_caveat: Russian PIAAC Cycle 1 sample EXCLUDED Moscow municipal area — under-represents the high-skill segment that is most relevant to this framework; PIAAC_gap_penalty for RU should be MAX (no Cycle 2) AND noted Moscow-exclusion warning even when Cycle 1 data is used
derived_indicators.confidence: high methodologically; low practically until pseudo-vectors are built and validated
derived_indicators.v05_CN_update: Use Xu et al. 2020 as a B-level prototype: NOCC descriptions + ML mapping to O*NET-style 161 skills/knowledge/abilities. Use Xu et al. 2021 Nature HSS Comm (peer-reviewed) as primary cite, not the 2020 arXiv preprint.
derived_indicators.v05_RU_update: Use profstandards as B/C-level NLP input: трудовые функции, трудовые действия, знания, умения. Apply confidence penalty until validated. No peer-reviewed Russian NLP work on профстандарты→skill embeddings exists as of May 2026 — I04_CCS_RU stays at C.
derived_indicators.v05_country_levels: A (US, O*NET 30.2 four-level Job Zone since 24 Feb 2026), A (EU, ESCO v1.2.1 from 10 Dec 2025), B (CN, Xu et al. 2021), C (RU, profstandards as NLP input only, unvalidated)

derived_indicators.name: GeographicReturnCapacityScore
derived_indicators.confidence: medium for US (NBER+LEHD); medium for EU; low for CN; low for RU (post-2022 emigration constraints make Russian case harder to instrument)
derived_indicators.v05_volume_estimates: 300K-1M+ Russian post-2022 emigrants depending on wave. First wave (Feb-Mar 2022): 300-400K. Second wave (post-Sept-2022 mobilization): additional 400-700K. Total: ~700K-1.3M with high uncertainty. Sectoral concentration: ~45% IT, ~15% culture/media, ~15% science/teaching, ~8% journalism. Sources: RANEPA Florinskaya, Stanford CDDRL OutRush 2024 longitudinal.
derived_indicators.v05_RU_evidence_level: C for point estimates (no Rosstat panel); B for ranges (RANEPA + OutRush survey-based). RU government 'IT 85% return' claim NOT corroborated by independent demographers.

derived_indicators.name: VerticalReturnCapacityScore
derived_indicators.confidence: medium for US tech; low elsewhere
derived_indicators.evidence_level_overall: C (limited by Raelin C-level + stigma signal which is qualitative)
v05_added_items: Returning from management to a senior IC/expert role is seen as legitimate, not a demotion.
Senior IC/expert tracks have compensation and status comparable to managerial tracks.
A period as IC/expert after management would not reduce future eligibility for management.
Hiring managers understand manager↔IC switches as skill-preserving rather than signal-damaging.
v05_supplementary_practitioner_C: Petroni & Colacino (2008) J Tech Mgmt Innov 3(3): 21-32 — foundational dual-ladder critique
Allen & Katz (1986) R&D Management — original critique
levels.fyi / Blind app — aggregate IC↔manager comp data
derived_indicators.v05_country_coverage: B for US/EU; C for CN/RU (no validated multi-country instrument for track-switch stigma)

derived_indicators.name: IntegratedCareerAgencyScore
derived_indicators.principle: Do not make agency purely psychological. A person can score high on confidence/adaptability but low on FEASIBLE agency if labour-market liquidity, credential portability or legal constraints block exit/return.
derived_indicators.structure: individual_resources × conversion_factors × opportunity_structure × constraints (per Sen-Nussbaum capability approach via Anand-Hunter-Smith 2005)
derived_indicators.confidence: low — composite of mixed-evidence components; requires extensive validation
derived_indicators.evidence_level_overall: B for US/EU (multiple validated components); C for CN/RU (no validated cross-cultural instruments)
derived_indicators.validation_strategy: must include sensitivity_analysis comparing scores with/without structural bottleneck multipliers — exposes how much agency is psychological vs institutional

v05_validated_components.limitation: fresh 2025 — international invariance not yet established

derived_indicators.v05_deep_research_critical_caveat: Bottleneck rule: I07_ICAS confidence cannot exceed C for CN/RU because none of the four patch instruments (CFI-R, Work Capability Set, CARINAS, Career Sustainability Scale) has documented psychometric invariance for those jurisdictions. CAAS (Savickas-Porfeli 2012, 13-country invariance) is the only instrument with broad cross-cultural foundation. Adding Hirschi-Wilhelm 2025 CSM-C as a Western-validated supplement does not change the CN/RU bottleneck.

light_survey_module.purpose: Minimal add-on to make public labour-market proxies individual-level without trying to solve all gaps with one long questionnaire.

items: I could return to a salaried role comparable to my last employee role within 6 months.
My current independent/freelance/founder work produces evidence employers in my prior field would value.
At least three clients, collaborators or former colleagues could refer me into a salaried role.
My licenses, credentials or certifications needed for my prior field are still valid.
I have maintained active professional ties in the field/geography/industry I might return to.
The cost of returning would be manageable for my household within the next 12 months.
I have recent, visible work output that is legible to employers in my prior industry.
My prior industry still recognizes my current experience as relevant.
I know the current hiring channels and qualification screens in my prior industry.
If I moved from management to an IC/expert role, I could demonstrate current hands-on skill.
If I moved from an IC/expert role to management, I could demonstrate recent people/project leadership.
My field has credible senior IC and managerial tracks with comparable status and compensation.
Returning from management to a senior IC/expert role is seen as legitimate, not a demotion.
Senior IC/expert tracks have compensation and status comparable to managerial tracks.
A period as IC/expert after management would not reduce future eligibility for management.
Hiring managers understand manager↔IC switches as skill-preserving rather than signal-damaging.
modules.split_construct: VR_07 split into structural_track_parity + perceived_track_switch_stigma per v0.5 patch

light_survey_module.scoring_note: Items use 5-point Likert; module score is the mean. Survey module is an ESSENTIAL augmentation — the 7 derived indicators cannot reach individual-level granularity from public administrative data alone.
light_survey_module.estimated_full_module_count_with_remaini: ~28 items total when extended with: 4 items for D03/D07 exit propensity, 4 items for D01 geographic exit, 4 items for psychological agency (CCQ-derived short form for A2/A3), 4 items for A4 network capital localization.
light_survey_module.v05_note: Added S5 track-switch stigma module (4 items) per patch recommendation. Total items now 32 (was 28). Maps to I06_VRCS split fields.

do_not_overclaim: Do not describe the 7 derived indicators (I01-I07) as VALIDATED instruments — they are operational proxies awaiting validation
Do not merge CN/RU pseudo-task-vectors into O*NET/PIAAC data without confidence penalties (I04_CCS)
Do not treat return PROBABILITY as return VALUE — probability of return can be high because the destination failed, not because return is attractive
Do not treat psychological agency as sufficient for realized mobility (per I07_ICAS principle)
Do not claim no prior work measures career mobility at all — Robinson 2018, Bechichi et al. 2018-2019, Amanzadeh-Kermani-McQuade 2024 all measure components
Do not treat country-level indices (Cedefop ESI) as individual-level agency instruments
implementation_notes.recommended_language_for_methodology_art: The framework is best positioned as an integrating operational architecture: it organises existing measurement infrastructures (O*NET, ESCO, PIAAC, BLS CWS, LEHD J2J, NBER hazard models, ISCO-aligned classifiers) around a 10-dimension exit/return matrix and proposes 7 derived indicators that close identified gaps via proxy scores. The resulting model is operationally usable but requires validation before being presented as a validated index.

validation_strategy.description: Test whether each derived indicator score predicts observed exit/return events in longitudinal data (LEHD J2J, SIPP, LFS-Long).
Test whether predicted high-capacity transitions preserve or improve wage, job quality, employment stability and subjective agency.
Test whether items and scores mean the same thing in US/EU/CN/RU; where they do not, report country-specific modules rather than forced comparisons.

Run scores with and without structural bottleneck multipliers to expose how much agency is psychological versus institutional. Aggregation: geometric mean preferred over additive when hard bottlenecks (legal access, credential recognition, missing demand) are present.

implementation_notes.aggregation_warning: When a dimension has hard bottlenecks such as legal access, credential recognition or missing demand, geometric means and caps are preferable to simple additive scores. This applies particularly to A7 (institutional constraints) and to RU/CN country scores where post-2022 / regulated-profession gates dominate.
implementation_notes.evidence_level_inheritance_rule: When a derived indicator combines multiple sources of mixed evidence levels, the indicator's confidence inherits from the WEAKEST ESSENTIAL signal (bottleneck rule). Indicator-level evidence_level_overall in derived_indicators reflects this rule.

v05_deep_research_additions.I07_ICAS_bottleneck_caveat: Bottleneck rule applied strictly: I07_ICAS confidence cannot exceed C for CN/RU because no instrument in the patch (CFI-R, Work Capability Set, CARINAS, Career Sustainability Scale) has documented psychometric invariance for those jurisdictions. CAAS (Savickas-Porfeli 2012, 13-country invariance) and CCQ (Akkermans et al. 2013) are the only instruments with broader cross-cultural foundation, but RU and PRC are not in their original validation samples either.
v05_deep_research_additions.I02_ROVS_anchor_change: Baldi-Trigeorgis 2020 (Journal of Intellectual Capital) DOWNGRADED from B/C to C — it is a Fortune-500 firm-level case study, not an individual return-option-value scale. PRIMARY admin-data anchor for I02_ROVS realized option-value is now Failla-Melillo-Reichstein 2017 JBV (Danish IDA matched employer-employee microdata). PRIMARY individual-level scale anchor is Hirschi-Wilhelm 2025 CSM-C (Career Self-Management Competencies).
v05_deep_research_additions.RU_credential_dual_layer_reminder: Russia profstandards have a TWO-LAYER architecture that v0.5 stores explicitly: (1) Постановление Правительства № 792 от 12 июня 2024 — REMOVES обязательные требования профстандартов from regulatory-guillotine exclusion list (effect from 13 June 2024). (2) Приказ Минтруда № 359 от 18 июля 2024 — regulates HOW to develop/update profstandards but does NOT restore mandatory force. Letter № 14-3/ООГ-4236 от 11 июля 2024 confirms: несоблюдение профстандартов не может быть основанием для административной ответственности (with narrow exceptions for accountants under FZ № 402-FZ where binding nature comes from a separate federal law).
v05_deep_research_additions.RU_return_migration_estimate_caveat: Russian post-2022 emigration volume estimates are inherently contested (300K–1M+ depending on wave and methodology). The bottleneck rule means I05_GRCS_RU stays at C for any point estimate, B for ranges. Best non-state sources: RANEPA (Florinskaya, Makarentseva, Mkrtchyan, Khasanova 2023 SSRN) and Stanford CDDRL OutRush 2024 longitudinal.
v05_deep_research_additions.BLS_CWS_2025_status: BLS Contingent Worker Supplement May 2025 was collected on schedule but results not officially released as of Federal Register notice 10 Feb 2026. July 2026 next CWS proposed with consolidated 24-question digital-platform module. v0.5 baseline for D10_US remains July 2023 CWS (4.3% contingent / 7.4% IC) until 2025 release.

gaps_residual.description: Even with CWS+SIPP+LEHD+CCQ bundle, no single ready instrument tracks employee→independent→employee bidirectional transitions at individual level for the same person across 3+ years. Needs purpose-built panel.
gaps_residual.v05_finding: SIPP, UKHLS/Understanding Society, SOEP, HILDA and EU-LFS longitudinal can observe realized self-employment/freelance-to-wage transitions. They do not directly measure capacity to return to a comparable salaried role.

gaps_residual.description: Real-options theory is rich (Luehrman 1998 C-level; Baldi D-level; academic real-options literature B-level for firms) but no validated individual-level instrument operationalises return optionality numerically. I02_ROVS is a proxy structure, not a validated scale.
gaps_residual.v05_finding: Baldi & Trigeorgis 2020 quantify career-development option value at organization/HR-program level. CFI-R career agency, Work Capability Set, Career Inaction Scale, and Career Sustainability Scale can serve as validated subcomponents, but no validated individual return-option-value instrument was found.

gaps_residual.description: Signal-preservation across industries (publications/portfolios/clearance) is field-specific and lacks a unified scale. Each domain (academia, finance, regulated professions) has its own marker set.
gaps_residual.v05_finding: Domain-specific signal registries exist: publications/patents for academia, FINRA BrokerCheck/U4/U5 for finance, revolving-door datasets for public-private transitions, professional licensing for regulated professions. A universal signal-preservation score remains unvalidated.

gaps_residual.description: State-published O*NET-equivalents do not exist for CN/RU. Pseudo-vectors via NLP on profstandards (RU) and occupation dictionary entries (CN) are feasible but not built. Xu et al. 2020 (CN academic prototype) is a research-grade starting point.
gaps_residual.v05_finding: China has an academic O*NET-mapped skill-taxonomy prototype predicting 161 O*NET-style skills/knowledge/abilities from Chinese occupation descriptions. Russia still lacks a comparable validated public task-vector layer; profstandards can support NLP pseudo-vectors.

gaps_residual.description: Lisbon RC nominally ratified by Russia (2000) but practical operation degraded after Russia ceased CoE membership in March 2022. Profstandards weakened as binding rule by Постановление № 792 от 13 июня 2024. Russian credential portability is theoretically present but practically constrained — neither v0.3 nor v0.4 fully resolves how to score this in I05_GRCS for RU.
gaps_residual.v05_finding: Council of Europe exclusion and LRC participation restrictions confirm practical-operational constraints. 2024 Mintrud recommendations keep profstandards useful as structured occupation-content artifacts, not general binding credential gates.

gaps_residual.description: VR_07 track-switch stigma signal in I06_VRCS is essentially qualitative. No validated scale of perceived stigma against IC↔manager track switches.
gaps_residual.v05_finding: Dual-ladder literature provides measurable constructs such as perceived equality/parity of technical and managerial tracks. Stigma of manager↔IC return remains under-measured and should be captured by added survey items.

gaps_residual.description: I07_ICAS combines components of mixed evidence levels (CCQ A + Anand A + LEHD B + Raelin C) into a single composite score. Composite validation requires concurrent measurement of all components on a substantial multi-country sample. Not available.
gaps_residual.v05_finding: Component-level validated scales are stronger than v0.4 reflected: CFI-R, Work Capability Set, Career Sustainability Scale, Career Inaction Scale, CCQ/CAAS. No combined ICAS-style index linking psychological agency to structural constraints has been validated.

v05_residual_frontier: No single panel captures employee→freelance/founder/gig→employee with comparable-return quality, agency assets and psychological readiness together.
No universal industry re-entry signal-preservation metric; domain modules are required.
No validated public RU/CN O*NET-equivalent task-vector layer with official status.
No multi-country validation of the integrated agency index.
